stories.title: Thought there was enough maritime drama? Look to the Gulf of Finland.
stories.summary: Elisabeth Braw is a senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, author of the award-winning “Goodbye Globalization” and a regular columnist for POLITICO. Her new book, “ The Undersea War ,” is out later this year.    As the world anxiously watches the Strait of Hormuz, another maritime drama is taking place right here in Europe. This one, too, concerns geopolitics and a small body of water, though these ships don’t have to worry about drone strikes. They’re simply stuck, waiting — because i

stories.title: Europe should regulate Big Tech instead of banning kids from social media, Estonia says
stories.summary: The European Union should focus on regulating large social media platforms instead of imposing bans for minors to use them, Estonia’s Education Minister Kristina Kallas said on Friday. European countries like France , Denmark and Greece are ploughing ahead with measures to keep teens away from using social media, citing mental health harms to minors. A POLITICO European Pulse survey of six major EU countries found that three in four people supported social media bans. Estonia is the only E

stories.title: Orbán and rival Magyar hold rallies as Hungary election campaigns enter final stretch – as it happened
stories.summary: Opposition candidate Péter Magyar holds rally outside Budapest as Orbán visits Székesfehérvár ahead of Sunday election Orbán and Magyar trade accusations in last days of Hungary election campaign Ashifa Kassam and Flora Garamvolgyi in Budapest As a child growing up in Budapest, Péter Magyar had a poster of Viktor Orbán – at the time a leading figure in the country’s pro-democracy movement – hanging above his bed. Continue reading...

stories.title: Human rights groups decry US plan for Guantánamo camp for Cuban migrants
stories.summary: Exclusive: Dozens of organizations write to Congress after general announced plan to ‘deal with’ those fleeing any humanitarian crisis on the island Dozens of US and international human rights organizations are decrying the Trump administration ’s plans to establish a migrant “camp” for fleeing Cubans at the Guantánamo Bay military base if the island nation’s crisis worsens under pressure from the US, according to a letter to members of Congress on Friday. The 85 groups plan to submit the joint

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Final push for votes as challenger to Hungary's Orbán scents victory
stories.summary: As Péter Magyar's opposition movement leads in the polls, tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters fill Heroes' Square in Budapest.

stories.title: Orbán and Magyar trade accusations in last days of Hungary election campaign
stories.summary: Polls suggest lead for opposition candidate before vote on Sunday as both allege enlistment of foreign interference Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win? Viktor Orbán and his centre-right rival, Péter Magyar, have traded accusations of enlisting foreign interference in a high-stakes election that polls suggest could mark the end of the nationalist Hungarian prime minister’s 16 years in power. As the two leaders’ campaigns entered their final stages before this weekend’s v

stories.title: Survivors of Epstein’s abuse accuse Melania Trump of ‘shifting burden’ on to victims
stories.summary: Outrage from survivors follows first lady’s statement calling on Congress to hold public hearings with victims of Epstein’s abuse Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox More than a dozen survivors of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse have accused Melania Trump of “shifting the burden” on to them after she called on Congress to hold public hearings with victims of Epstein’s abuse. “Survivors of Jeffrey Epstein have already shown extraordinary courage by coming forward

stories.title: Even if Victor Orbán is ousted on Sunday, Hungary’s return to liberal democracy is not guaranteed | Gabriela Greilinger and Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Challenger Péter Magyar is no progressive – and after 16 years of creeping authoritarianism, the PM has embedded Fidesz in the Hungarian state On Sunday, Hungarians will go to the polls to decide on their country’s direction for the next four years in an election that looks as if it will be a nail-biter. Viktor Orbán, Europe’s longest-serving prime minister – who has been in power for 16 years and transformed his country into an electoral autocracy – could lose the election . Ahead of the vote,

stories.title: ’He’s mentally unstable’: Iranian American in Congress condemns Trump’s war and pushes for his removal
stories.summary: Democratic representative Yassamin Ansari says the war has only more deeply entrenched the Iranian regime Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Donald Trump is an “evil human being” who “wants to be an emperor” and should be removed from office over the war in Iran, Yassamin Ansari , an Iranian American member of the US Congress , has told the Guardian. Ansari, the daughter of Iranian immigrants who decades ago fled the regime, spoke out after the presiden

stories.title: German transgender far-right extremist arrested in Czech Republic
stories.summary: German media reports say Marla-Svenja Liebich used to be a member of a neo-Nazi group called Blood and Honour.

stories.title: German Bundestag chief visits Baltic states to discuss cyber defence on NATO's eastern flank
stories.summary: Klöckner will also visit the German Bundeswehr brigade in Lithuania on Thursday. Around 5,000 German soldiers are to be deployed to Lithuania by 2027 to support the local military.

stories.title: Hungary: Orban and opposition trade barbs ahead of election
stories.summary: Hungary is preparing for a potentially pivotal election, with challenger Peter Magyar and the Tisza party promising change after 16 years of Viktor Orban's rule. Polling shows nearly a quarter of voters are undecided.

stories.title: Poland: "Frog Patrol" saves thousands of amphibians during migration
stories.summary: Volunteers near Warsaw form a frog patrol each spring, helping thousands of frogs and toads cross a road safely and protect local breeding grounds.

stories.title: Elections in Hungary: What do the polls say?
stories.summary: Medián, currently considered among the most accurate pollsters in Hungary, predicts a two-thirds majority in parliament for opposition party Tisza. Most other pollsters estimate a smaller lead for them, while few have the ruling Fidesz party narrowly ahead.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor szerint nem létezik a csádi misszió
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök harmadszor adott interjút Rónai Egonnak. Wáberer Györgynél a toronyban van baj, mondta.

stories.title: Balázs Péter: Orbán meséje a kisegérről és az oroszlánnak nevezett Oroszországról az alávetettség csúcsa
stories.summary: A volt külügyminiszter szerint a magyar kormány az EU alapszerződését is megsértette azzal, ahogyan eljár a szövetségben orosz szívességeket teljesítve. Egy egykori orosz diplomata úgy látja, hogy ha nem is tipikus, ahogyan a magyar külügyminiszter alárendeli magát az orosznak, a mai diplomáciában Trump megjelenése óta is felborult a protokoll.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor Székesfehérváron: Mindenki hozzon el mindenkit
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök utolsó előtti, székesfehérvári kampánygyűlésén felidézte legendássá vált 2002-es felszólítását. Úgy tűnik, fejenként még egy ember most szerinte se lenne elég.

stories.title: Molotov-koktélt dobtak az OpenAI vezérigazgatójának házára
stories.summary: Az elkövető azzal fenyegetőzött, hogy felgyújtja a techcég San Franciscó-i központját.

stories.title: Eddig 113 ezer levélszavazat érkezett be Romániából és 46 ezer Szerbiából
stories.summary: A többi országból jóval kevesebb, Németországból például hétezer, Nagy-Britanniából kevesebb mint háromezer.

stories.title: Toroczkai László: Ma már kiderült, hogy hazudott Magyar Péter
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke nemrég azt állította, hallotta, hogy a Mi Hazánk több tucat képviselőjelöltje fog visszalépni a Fidesz javára. Toroczkai szerint ez nem jött be.

stories.title: A becsület nem üres szó, hanem azt jelenti, hogy kiállunk az igazság mellett, akkor is, amikor nehéz
stories.summary: „Megtisztelő, hogy önöket szolgálhattam” – mondta a Rendszerbontó Nagykoncerten Pálinkás Szilveszter, aki talán utoljára lépett egyenruhában a nyilvánosság elé. „Ebben a rendszerben alkotmányos alapjogaink se tudnak érvényesülni, ennek véget kell vetni” – mondta Szabó Bence rendőrszázados.

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: Több minden is arra utal, hogy tényleg omladozik a NER
stories.summary: A kampány finisében olyan történéseknek lehetünk szemtanúi, amelyekre eddig még egyáltalán nem volt példa. Rendőr, katona, extitkosszolga teregeti ki a rezsim szennyesét, és olyan helyekről szivárognak információk, amelyekről korábban elképzelhetetlen lett volna.

stories.title: Írországban autópályákat zárnak le az üzemanyagár miatt tüntetők
stories.summary: Csütörtökön a dublini reptérre is gyalogolniuk kellett az utasoknak. A rendőrség blokádként kezeli a helyzetet.

stories.title: A Trollfoci szerkesztője, Antal Adrián az MTVA Online igazgatója lett
stories.summary: Kinevezése közel fél éve megtörtént, de eddig valahogy a radar alatt maradt.

stories.title: A jó politikus inkább ne keresse az igazságot?
stories.summary: Oidipusz jó miniszterelnök lett volna. De nem lesz, a választás estéjén ugyanis kipotyognak a múlt csontvázai a szekrényből. Szophoklész drámáját Robert Icke aktualizálta 2018-ban, Magyarországon elsőként a Radnóti Színházban mutatták be 2024-ben. Idén a választás estéjén is műsorra tűzik, nem véletlenül. Kritika.

stories.title: Puzsér: 1956 óta nem tették ennyien személyes ügyükké, hogy az ország sorsa Keletre vagy Nyugatra vezet majd
stories.summary: A publicista a Telexnek elmondta, hogyan szerveződött meg a pénteki Rendszerbontó Nagykoncert, és arról is beszélt, az, ahogy 2024 februárja óta a magyarok hozzáállnak a politikához, már polgári társadalmat idéz.

stories.title: Magyar Péterék úgy vernek köröket a Fideszre a TikTokon, hogy sokkal kevesebbet posztolnak
stories.summary: A tiszás oldalak harmadannyi poszttal értek el több megosztást és kedvelést, mint a kormánypárthoz köthető profilok.

stories.title: Poland's Catholic Church prepares parishes as crisis centres amid war fears
stories.summary: The Polish Bishops' Conference has established a special working group to develop an action plan in the event of an armed conflict reaching Poland, as "most Poles will first turn to the church for help, and only then to municipal institutions and offices," officials say.

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Serbia, Hungary say explosives found at Russian gas pipeline
stories.summary: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said detonators and explosives "of devastating power" were found near the Balkan Stream pipeline, which runs through Serbia and provides Russian gas to Hungary.

stories.title: In Estonia, rumors are circulating that the city of Narva wants to secede and join Russia. Is it true?
stories.summary: The Estonian city of Narva is on Europe's eastern border, right next to Russia. Recently there have been online rumors that some locals want to secede from Estonia and join Russia. What's really happening?

stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Ukraine insists US still an ally despite rising tensions
stories.summary: The United States remains an ally even as tensions between Kyiv and Washington have escalated in recent months, Ukraine’s ambassador to the EU said. “Indeed, America is an ally for Ukraine,” Vsevolod Chentsov told POLITICO’s European Pulse Forum. Chentsov struck a cautious tone throughout, emphasizing that Ukraine “still rely[ies] on U.S. help on air defense, and it’s critical for us,” while also highlighting growing cooperation with European partners and Ukraine’s own expanding drone capabiliti

stories.title: After clashing with Trump and Israel, Sánchez casts Spain as moral model for EU
stories.summary: BARCELONA — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez on Friday urged Europe to “dream big” and not only defend its interests, but become the moral leader in an increasingly turbulent world. “Europe’s challenge is not only to rearm itself to address its security and defense problems, but to also rearm itself morally, so that it can contribute to stable and peaceful development throughout the world,” Sánchez said at POLITICO’s European Pulse Forum in Barcelona. The prime

stories.title: Four men deported by US to Eswatini have right to see lawyer, court rules
stories.summary: The men, sent to the southern African country in July, have been denied in-person counsel for nine months Four men deported by the US to Eswatini and denied in-person legal counsel for nine months while detained in a maximum security prison have the right to see a local lawyer, Eswatini’s supreme court ruled. The men, from Cambodia, Cuba, Vietnam and Yemen, were sent to the small southern African country , formerly known as Swaziland, in July despite having no connection to the country, as part

stories.title: Remaining in Nato is in best interests of US, says Keir Starmer
stories.summary: PM pushes back after Trump’s threats to leave alliance and says European members must do more in light of Iran war Keir Starmer has said it is in the best interests of the US to stay in Nato and that Europe must do more to support the alliance in light of the war in Iran. The British prime minister, speaking at the end of a multi-stop trip around the Gulf to discuss the tentative ceasefire and options to fully reopen the commercially vital strait of Hormuz, pushed back against Donald Trump’s thr

stories.title: Ukraine : Russian drone attacks hit Sumy and Odesa as Easter truce looms
stories.summary: Russian drone strikes hit Sumy and Odesa, injuring two and damaging homes and energy sites, as Putin proposes a brief Easter ceasefire.

stories.title: EU industry chief says Europe won’t follow the US on China
stories.summary: The European Union would benefit from more Chinese investment and shouldn’t follow the U.S.’s isolationist strategy, said Industry Commissioner Stéphane Séjourné Friday. Speaking at POLITICO’s European Pulse Forum in Barcelona, the top EU official identified “a path forward for Chinese investment” in the bloc. “We need it. We need foreign investment.” The Commission vice president explained that the EU had chosen to “de-risk its economy.” &#8

stories.title: Le Pen met France’s richest man and other major CEOs at secret dinner
stories.summary: PARIS — Marine Le Pen dined with LVMH chief Bernard Arnault and other influential French corporate leaders on Tuesday, a meeting that offers another indication her far-right party, the National Rally, has become a mainstream movement that is no longer ostracized by the business community. TotalEnergies Chief Executive Patrick Pouyanné and several other leaders of companies listed on the CAC40, France’s main stock index, also attended, according to a senior National Rally official and

stories.title: UK to host officials for Strait of Hormuz talks next week
stories.summary: LONDON — The U.K. will host a fresh round of talks with non-U.S. allies on how to unblock the Strait of Hormuz next week, a British official with knowledge of the planning said Friday. Officials from 41 countries are due to convene for the first time since U.S. President Donald Trump announced a ceasefire with Iran, said the official, who was granted anonymity to discuss internal plans. Trump has piled pressure on NATO allies to present their own plans for ensuring freedom of navigation through

stories.title: UK starts crackdown on EU citizens’ post-Brexit rights
stories.summary: Concerns raised over use of travel data in determining whether people are ‘continuously’ in UK, after HMRC fiasco UK ministers are to start removing post-Brexit residency rights from EU citizens who are no longer “continuously” living in the country. The initiative is legal under the 2020 Brexit withdrawal agreement but the decision to use travel data to partly determine absences has raised concerns after the HMRC fiasco that saw almost 20,000 parents stripped of child benefits because of inaccu

stories.title: Concerns raised about motorbike tourist trail after death of British teenager in Vietnam
stories.summary: Local people say road conditions are rugged and weather unpredictable, while some say it has become too congested The recent death of a British gap-year student on the Ha Giang loop , a popular motorcycle tour through the mountains in north Vietnam, has heightened concerns about a trail reputed to be one of the most dangerous in the country. Orla Wates, 19, from Surrey, was riding as a pillion passenger when she fell off and was hit by an oncoming truck , according to local media. She was taken

stories.title: Labour MPs propose specialist sexual offences courts to help trials backlog
stories.summary: Rebels put forward amendments to courts bill in attempt to stop government plans to cut back on jury trials Labour MPs are hoping to hijack plans to cut back on jury trials in England and Wales by proposing specialist courts for sexual offences with fixed dates for trial. Those behind the amendment want to block the wider plan to stop thousands of cases being potentially eligible for jury trials – a measure ministers say is needed to cut court backlogs – and they say the specialist courts alone

stories.title: Crispin Odey drops £79m libel claim against FT over sexual misconduct allegations
stories.summary: Ex-hedge fund manager ‘forced to accept’ newspaper likely to succeed in its public interest defence, say his lawyers Crispin Odey, the former hedge fund manager, has dropped his £79m libel claim against the Financial Times over its reporting of sexual misconduct allegations against him, his lawyers have said. In 2023, the FT published several articles from 20 women alleging sexual assault and harassment against Odey, covering a period of five decades. He has previously denied the allegations aga

stories.title: JD Vance dispatched to negotiate Iran peace with few cards to play
stories.summary: Vice-president’s war doubts and his boss’s desperation to reopen Red Sea look like a weak deck against bolstered opponents As JD Vance arrives in Islamabad to negotiate a peace deal with Iran, his first high-profile assignment of the war looks to be a poisoned chalice. Vance, a vocal opponent of US wars in the Middle East gone quiet since the beginning of the current military campaign, will now face off with Iranian negotiators who feel emboldened by their new control of the Hormuz strait and th

stories.title: RMIT drops misconduct case against student who accused university of being ‘complicit in Gaza genocide’
stories.summary: Exclusive: Move comes after Guardian Australia revealed Gemma Seymour was facing potential suspension over video criticising RMIT’s ties to weapons companies RMIT University has dropped a misconduct case against a student who accused the institution of being “complicit in genocide” in Gaza , because of its defence and aerospace research centre’s ties to weapons companies. stories.title: Pressure on Swalwell to drop California governor bid amid sexual assault claims
stories.summary: Former allies of Democratic contender withdraw support after accusations in San Francisco Chronicle and on CNN California gubernatorial candidate Eric Swalwell is facing escalating pressure to drop out of the race following an allegation of sexual assault and multiple allegations of sexual misconduct. The San Francisco Chronicle published on Friday an account of a woman who said she was sexually assaulted by Swalwell on two separate occasions. Continue reading...

stories.summary: ‘Predatory industry’ adept at pivoting quickly to target new mediums and markets, says independent senator David Pocock Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor’s plan for a partial ban on gambling advertising is ripe for exploitation, say experts and crossbench MPs, including David Pocock, warning rules applying to podcasters, social media platforms and influencers need to be tightened. stories.title: Fears of UK and EU flight cancellations as airports warn of jet fuel shortages
stories.summary: Summer holidays could be hit unless oil flows through strait of Hormuz recommence within three weeks Airports have said jet fuel could run short within three weeks in Europe if oil supplies do not start to flow through the strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over flight cancellations in the UK and EU going into the summer holiday season. Jet fuel shortages will become so acute without the resumption of supplies from the Middle East that cancellations across Europe will be inevitable, disrupting t

stories.title: Man arrested after four die trying to cross Channel in small boat
stories.summary: Two men and two women were swept away by currents while attempting to board dinghy off French coast A man has been arrested on suspicion of endangering life after four people died in a small boat Channel crossing on Thursday. The man, described by prosecutors as a 27-year-old Sudanese national, was arrested by National Crime Agency investigators on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patron The Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded. The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. Continue reading...

stories.title: Reform UK local election candidate was twice disciplined by Tories over ‘racist comments’
stories.summary: Conservatives disowned Derek Bullock in 2023 after he allegedly used racial slur to call for people of Pakistani heritage to be shot A Reform UK candidate for next month’s council elections was twice disciplined by the Conservatives over alleged offensive or racist comments, while another shared conspiracy theories about Covid, it has emerged, as the full slate of candidates was confirmed. More than 5,000 council places in England are being contested on 7 May, along with several mayoralties, and

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, Russian shelling in Ukraine, Artemis’s lunar flyby and World Press Photo winners – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Romford boy, 16, charged with murder of Eghosa Ogbebor, 14, in south London
stories.summary: Shooting took place near Woolwich Dockyard train station, and another 16-year-old boy remains in custody A 16-year-old boy from Romford has been charged with the murder of Eghosa Ogbebor, 14, who was shot dead in south-east London last week, the Metropolitan police has said. Another 16-year-old was arrested on suspicion of murder on Friday and remains in custody, the force added. Continue reading...

stories.title: Repurposed drug may extend survival in aggressive ovarian cancer, trial shows
stories.summary: Relacorilant, typically used to treat Cushing’s syndrome, could improve outcomes in platinum-resistant cases A drug originally used to treat a rare disease could extend the lives of patients with an aggressive form of ovarian cancer, according to a clinical trial. Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer occurs when the disease progresses within six months of starting platinum-based chemotherapy. This form of chemotherapy is different from other types because it uses compounds that contain platinum to

stories.title: Fuel-price protests cause chaos in Ireland and spread to Norway
stories.summary: Irish drivers told to buy only fuel they need as filling stations run out and hauliers and farmers block motorways in fourth day of action Middle East crisis live – latest updates Protests over fuel prices have caused chaos in Ireland and spread to Norway in a knock-on effect from the conflict in the Middle East . People in Ireland were urged to only buy fuel they needed as 100 fuel stations ran out, and the National Emergency Coordination Group warned the number could rise to 500 on Friday. Con

stories.title: Multiple people face charges, including murder, in California fireworks blast
stories.summary: Massive fire followed explosion at 5,000-sq-ft warehouse near Esparto, an hour from Sacramento, on 1 July Multiple people have been charged with murder in connection to a fatal fireworks-warehouse explosion in California that killed seven people and injured two others in July. The explosion at the 5,000-sq-ft warehouse sparked a massive fire near the small town of Esparto, about an hour outside Sacramento. stories.title: Artemis II crew splashes down in Pacific Ocean, ending record-breaking moon flyby
stories.summary: The four astronauts touched down on Earth off the coast of California, concluding historic 10-day mission Artemis II splashdown – live updates The Artemis II, and the four astronauts aboard the Orion space capsule, has splashed down into the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego, Nasa has confirmed. The spacecraft touched down at 5.07pm (1.07am BST) making the journey around the moon and back officially 9 days 1hr and 32min. The Orion spacecraft traveled 694,481 miles (1,117,659km), Nasa said

stories.summary: Australia and Singapore will ‘make maximum efforts to meet each other’s energy security needs’ in refined fuels and LNG, according to new agreement Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s largest petrol source has pledged not to cut supplies, with Singapore’s prime minister telling Anthony Albanese that fuel will keep flowing despite the international crisis. stories.title: Peruvians to go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability

About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congre

stories.summary: As the US and Iran gear up for high-stakes talks, the bigger question is whether Tehran can hold the line amid signs of fractures within the regime.

The painter, now credited with pioneering the abstract art movement , did not seek recognition after peers rejected her avant garde works. Instead, she ordered that they be hidden for 20 years after her death and never sold. Cont

stories.summary: Kiemelt beruházássá nyilvánították a projektet egy késő esti rendeletben.

stories.title: Ezekért érdemes moziba menni április 10-től április 17-ig
stories.summary: Itt a következő hét napra szóló Telex Diszpó.

stories.title: 135 év után nem lesz szakszervezeti majális a Városligetben, a Mészároshoz köthető Indamedia kapta meg a területet
stories.summary: A Városliget Zrt. azt írta, hogy az Indamedia előbb adta be kérelmet, mint a szakszervezetek, és ez döntött a területhasználati engedély kiadásakor.

stories.title: Barátság kőolajvezeték: Zelenszkij megígérte az EU-nak, hogy még tavasszal befejezik a javítást
stories.summary: Az ukrán elnök azt mondta, már sok mindent elvégeztek.

stories.title: Dömötör Csaba: A Fidesz Demokrácia Központot hoz létre a tiszás választási csalások megakadályozására
stories.summary: A fideszes európai parlamenti képviselő állítása szerint a tiszások erőszakosan lépnek fel fideszes aktivistákkal szemben.

stories.title: Élő bejelentkezések, azonnal frissülő adatok, térkép, stúdiós interjúk – ilyen lesz a Telex eddigi legnagyobb választási műsora
stories.summary: Április 12-én lapunk történetének eddigi legnagyobb választási műsorával készülünk, hogy olvasóink és nézőink egész nap hiteles képet kaphassanak a történésekről.

stories.title: A kórházigazgatók is elismerték, amit az adatok mutatnak az egészségügyről
stories.summary: A kórházigazgatói pályázatok szerint az intézményvezetők is látnak számos olyan problémát, amelyekről eddig is árulkodtak az adatok, de a kormány igyekezett ezeket tagadni.

stories.title: Politico: Sértések és fenyegetések sora hangzott el Trump és a NATO-főtitkár találkozóján
stories.summary: A lap értesülései szerint nem sikerült jól a megbeszélés, Trump fenyegetőzött, megtorlást is kilátásba helyezett.

stories.title: Stefano Gabbana már nem a Dolce & Gabbana igazgatója
stories.summary: A cég szerint ez nem befolyásolja a kreatív feladatait. Több mint negyven éve alapította a céget Domenico Dolcéval.

stories.title: Rendszerbontó Nagykoncert: Hülyének vagyunk nézve, és mindig azt hiszik, hogy mindent elhiszünk nekik
stories.summary: Több mint ötven fellépője van a Puzsér-féle rendszerbontó koncertnek. A Telex választási műsora élőben jelentkezett a Hősök teréről, ahol Bíró-Nagy Andrással is beszélgettünk a kampány aktualitásairól.

stories.title: Olyan cég utalt milliárdokat Mészáros Lőrincnek, amelyből már korábban kiszállt
stories.summary: Volt tulajdonosnak nem fizethet osztalékot egy vállalat, de nem ez az egyetlen furcsaság ebben a történetben.

stories.title: 241 milliárd osztalék kifizetéséről döntött a Mol
stories.summary: Részvényenként 180 forint alaposztalék és 120 forint rendkívüli osztalék jár.

stories.title: Palácsik-Ráthonyi Tímea megosztotta Wáberer György és Balogh Levente választási biztatását is
stories.summary: A modell többet nem mondott, de a Fidesz ellen felszólaló hírességek videóit megosztotta.

stories.title: Soha többé vodkanarancs! – Puzsér rímbe szedte a Rendszerbontók dühét
stories.summary: A Rendszerbontó Nagykoncert szevezője rendesen feltüzelte közönségét a ritmusos felsorolásával. Előtte Pálinkás Szilvesztert és Szabó Bencét is sztárként ünnepelték, előbbi azt mondta, hogy a bátorság az, ha nem hagyjuk, hogy a hazugság és a félelem határozzák meg az irányt.

stories.title: 22 éves olasz hercegnő a barátnője Marine Le Pen potenciális szélsőjobboldali utódjának
stories.summary: Bourbon-Szicíliai Mária Karolina a Napkirály távoli leszármazottja, ma már nem bír hivatalos jogi jelentéssel a címe, viszont nagyon gazdag, és várhatóan a család következő vezetője lesz.

stories.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
stories.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

stories.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
stories.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

stories.title: Migrant dinghy sinks in English Channel off France, 4 people die
stories.summary: A boat carrying more than 40 people ran into trouble early in the morning, with two men and two women dying in the waters. Crossings are building up after the winter lull, but the sea temperatures are slower to rise.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia's Putin declares Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Russian President Putin has announced a ceasefire in its war in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter holiday. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said his country will "act accordingly" during the truce.

stories.title: Watch: BBC asks Artemis II crew a question in space
stories.summary: The BBC’s Science Editor Rebecca Morelle has spoken to the Artemis II crew ahead of their likely splashdown on Saturday.

stories.title: Ceasefire threatened as Iran closes strait again and Trump warns US troops are to remain
stories.summary: In a social media post, Trump said the surge of US warships and troops will remain around Iran “until such time as the real agreement reached is fully complied with.”

stories.title: Fiscal discipline as Ukraine's real test on the road to EU membership
stories.summary: Fiscal discipline will be Ukraine's real test on the road to EU membership, writes Danylo Hetmantsev, chair of parliament’s finance, tax and customs committee, in an op-ed for Euronews. He argues that Ukraine has already shown it can turn shadow economy reforms into real revenue.

stories.title: Israeli strikes on Beirut kill at least 182 and wound hundreds
stories.summary: Lebanon spent the night digging through rubble after Israeli strikes hit busy commercial and residential districts in central Beirut late on Wednesday, killing at least 182 people and wounding 890, according to the health ministry.

stories.title: Mexico: Miner rescued alive after 14 days trapped underground
stories.summary: After 14 days trapped in a flooded gold and silver mine, a worker has been pulled out alive while two others have died.

stories.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
stories.summary: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

stories.title: Berlin opens Süd Ost Galerie, a new open-air street art landmark
stories.summary: Germany has unveiled a new open‑air street art destination in Berlin, where the 450‑metre Süd Ost Galerie now stretches along Grenzweg in the south‑east of the capital.

stories.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon leave more than 200 dead and 1,000 wounded
stories.summary: Lebanon is searching for survivors after Israeli strikes on Beirut, Tyre and the southern village of Abbassiyeh killed at least 203 people and wounded more than 1,000, the health ministry said, as the overall death toll from attacks since early March surpasses 1,500.

stories.title: UK and Norway foil Russian submarine plot to survey undersea cables in north Atlantic
stories.summary: Britain and Norway announced new joint naval patrols aimed at protecting undersea cables from Russia in December, with a fleet of at least 13 warships protecting critical infrastructure in the North Atlantic.

stories.title: Pro-Iran groups using AI to troll Trump and try to control war narrative, analysts say
stories.summary: Analysts say the memes appear to be coming from groups linked to the government in Tehran and are part of a strategy of leveraging its limited resources to inflict damage on the US.

stories.title: Confusion reigns over Hormuz shipping reopening as Iran issues new passage conditions
stories.summary: Normal shipping in the Strait of Hormuz has not yet resumed as per the ceasefire deal, as Iran consolidates its hardline, unilateral position on lifting its blockade in apparent contradiction of the rollercoaster ceasefire deal.

stories.title: Iran rules out any restrictions on its enrichment programme, nuclear chief says
stories.summary: The issue of uranium enrichment has been central to Western relations with Iran for more than two decades, with the US and its allies accusing Tehran of seeking atomic weapons.

stories.title: UAE urges full compliance from Iran following ceasefire announcement
stories.summary: The UAE is seeking clarifications on the US-Iran ceasefire deal regarding attacks across the Gulf, as well as on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as a “central priority”, as Tehran's blockade continues despite its commitment to reopen the strait to free navigation.

stories.title: Trump again berates NATO, calls it 'disappointing'
stories.summary: Trump's outrage at NATO allies over their failure to join the Iran war had prompted fears he would seek to pull the United States out of the nearly eight-decade-old alliance.

stories.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

errors.error: <urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
<urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
